_meta.name: okta-assign-app-to-user
_meta.description: Assign an application to an Okta user. Navigates to the user's Applications tab on their profile page, clicks Assign Applications, and assigns the named app.
_meta.author: session-2026-04-14

auth.note: Okta OAuth PKCE — session must already be active.

userId.description: Okta user ID (format: 00u{alphanumeric}698). Get from list-users perceive mainActions.

app_name.description: Display name of the application to assign (e.g. 'Slack', 'Google Workspace')

anomalies: Applications tab href is #tab-apps on user profile page
App assignment may open a modal with filterable app list or a search field
Some apps require attribute mapping before assignment completes — perceive for any form that appears after app selection
Trial org may have limited apps — use 06-list-apps first to confirm app exists

chain.note: Confirm profile loaded. Check for Applications tab.
Click Applications tab to see currently assigned apps
Perceive Applications tab. Look for 'Assign Applications' button.
Open app assignment modal. Fallback: 'Assign', '+ Add Applications'.
Wait for app list modal to load
Perceive app assignment modal. Apps listed with Assign buttons per row.

Click Assign next to the target app. If multiple apps shown, the executor must locate the correct row. NOTE: This clicks the first 'Assign' button — may need row-scoped click if multiple apps present.
Check if an attribute form appeared (some apps require username/role mapping). If so, click 'Save and Go Back' or 'Done'.
ENABLE if attribute mapping form appeared. Fallback: 'Done', 'Next'.
Verify app appears in user's Applications tab.
